title: Systems and methods for converting methane to one or more products
abstract: The systems and methods convert methane to one or more products using a green rust material in spontaneous, electrocatalytic, and photochemical reactions under aerobic and anaerobic conditions. In an embodiment, a first electrode that includes a green rust material and a second electrode are contacted by an electrolyte, where the first and second electrodes are separated by an ion exchange membrane. For electrocatalytic reactions, a potential difference is applied across the electrodes. For photochemical reactions, the green rust material is illuminated. Liquid products (e.g., methanol and/or acetate) can be removed from the electrolyte in contact with the green rust material after the desired contact period.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system for converting methane to one or more products, comprising: a container comprising a first volume and a second volume adapted to hold a first electrolyte; an ion exchange membrane disposed in the container and separating the first volume from the second volume; a first electrode, comprising a quantity of a green rust material positioned in the first volume of the container to contact the first electrolyte; a second electrode positioned in the second volume of the container to form, together with the first electrode, electrical terminals of an electrochemical cell; and a source of methane configured to supply methane to the first volume. 2. The system of claim 1, further comprising a source of potential connected to the first and second electrodes and adapted to apply a potential difference across the electrodes. 3. The system as in claim 1 or 2, wherein the second electrode comprises platinum. 4. The system as in any of the preceding claims, wherein the first electrolyte comprises KHCO 3 . 5. The system as in any of the preceding claims, wherein the source of methane is adapted to provide a continuous supply of methane to the container. 6. The system as in any of the preceding claims, wherein the source of methane is adapted to supply a mixture of methane and oxygen. 7. The system as in any of claims 1-5, further comprising a source of oxygen configured to supply oxygen to the second volume. 8. The system as in any of claims 2-6, wherein the source of potential is adapted to apply a potential to the first electrode to create a voltage bias compared to a reference electrode, wherein the voltage bias comprises between about -1. 5 V and about -0.01 V under aerobic conditions. 9. The system as in any of claims 2-5 or 7, wherein the source of potential is adapted to apply a potential to the first electrode to create a voltage bias compared to a reference electrode, wherein the voltage bias comprises between about 0.0 V and about +1.5 V under anaerobic conditions. 10. The system as in any of the preceding claims, wherein the green rust material comprises at least one crystalline layer and wherein the at least one crystalline layer comprises Fe n -OH and Fe ni -OH. 11. The system as in any of the preceding claims, wherein the ratio of Fe n -OH to Fe ni -OH in the green rust material is between about 0.5 and about 2.5. 12. The system as in any of the preceding claims, wherein, as synthesized and prior to exposure to air, the at least one crystalline layer consists essentially of Fe n -OH and Fe ni -OH. 13. The system as in any of the preceding claims further comprising a light source configured to illuminate the green rust material. 14. A method for converting methane to one or more products, comprising the steps of supplying methane to an electrolyte; contacting a first electrode comprising a quantity of a green rust material with the electrolyte; contacting a second electrode with the electrolyte; separating the first electrode from the second electrode with an ion exchange membrane; and removing the electrolyte after contacting the first electrode for a contact period. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising a step of supplying oxygen to the electrolyte on the same side of the ion exchange membrane as the first electrode. 16. The method of claim 14, further comprising the step of supplying oxygen to the electrolyte on the same side of the ion exchange membrane as the second electrode. 17. The method as in any of claims 14-16, further comprising the steps of: contacting a reference electrode with the electrolyte on the same side of the ion exchange membrane as the first electrode; and applying a potential to the first electrode to create a voltage bias compared to the reference electrode. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the voltage bias comprises between about -1. 5 V and about -0.01 V under aerobic conditions. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the voltage bias comprises between about 0.0 V and about +1.5 V under anaerobic conditions. 20. The method as in any of claims 14-19, wherein the green rust material comprises at least one crystalline layer and wherein the at least one crystalline layer comprises Fe 11 - OH and Felll-OH. 21. The method as in any of claims 14-20, wherein the ratio of Fe u -OH to Fe in -OH in the green rust material is between about 0.5 and 2.5. 22. The method as in any of claims 14-21, wherein the electrolyte comprises KHCO3. 23. The method as in any of claims 14-22, wherein the second electrode comprises platinum. 24. The method as in any of claims 14-23, wherein the contact period is between about 10 minutes and 60 minutes. 25. The method as in any of claims 14-24, further comprising the step of forming the green rust material, comprising the steps of: 

 supplying N2 or Ar to a second electrolyte, wherein the second electrolyte comprises NaHCCh adjusted to a pH of about 9.5 by adding NaOH; contacting the first electrode and a counter electrode with the second electrolyte; and applying a potential difference across the first electrode and the counter electrode. 26. The method of claim 25 further comprising, before the step of contacting the first electrode and the counter electrode with the second electrolyte, a step of cleaning the first electrode. 27. The method as in any of claims 14-17 or 20-26, further comprising illuminating the green rust material.
